prompt: Find me papers on machine learning for fraud detection in accounting.
assertions: Returns at least 3 papers
Papers are ranked by relevance first
Each paper includes title, authors, year, journal, and a DOI or URL
High-relevance papers include a structured summary with RQ, method, findings
At least one key quote is provided in APA format
Results are drawn from target journals (JAR, JAE, TAR, CAR, RAS, AOS, JAPP, AJPT, AH)

prompt: What does the literature say about audit quality and client characteristics?
assertions: Interprets the implicit literature search request without requiring clarification
Returns papers from auditing-relevant journals (AJPT, TAR, JAR, etc.)
Papers are ranked relevance > citations > year
Recent papers appear before older ones within the same relevance/citation tier
Lower-relevance papers use the brief summary format

prompt: Find papers on ESG disclosure and greenwashing detection using NLP.
assertions: Conducts multiple searches to cover both ESG/greenwashing and NLP angles
Returns results from AOS, JAE, or other relevant target journals if available
Handles interdisciplinary scope (accounting + NLP) without getting confused
Offers to search more broadly if fewer than 3 papers found
Quotes are clearly from accessible content (abstract or open-access)

prompt: Look up research on going concern opinions and bankruptcy prediction.
assertions: Returns papers from AJPT, TAR, or JAR as primary sources
Citation counts are included where available
Papers are in ranked order
Offers follow-up options at the end

prompt: What papers exist on earnings management around IPOs?
assertions: Returns highly cited, well-known papers (e.g., Teoh et al.) if accessible
Ranking correctly prioritizes relevance over recency for this classic topic
APA-style in-text citation is used for any quoted text
Includes DOI or journal link for each paper
